objective: Test 10 different enforcement mechanisms to prevent commit-forgetting

solution_1.name: Hook-based reminder
solution_1.approach: settings.json hook that logs reminders after file write

solution_2.name: Persistent background agent
solution_2.approach: Long-running agent that monitors git state and alerts

solution_3.name: Skill wrapper
solution_3.approach: Custom skill that wraps file operations with git commit

solution_4.name: Context window inflation
solution_4.approach: Embed rule in system message with explicit token cost penalties

solution_6.name: MCP server state tracking
solution_6.approach: MCP resource that tracks last committed state

solution_7.name: File watcher script
solution_7.approach: Background bash script monitoring uncommitted changes

solution_8.name: Token cost visibility
solution_8.approach: Track and display cost of each forgotten commit (~100 tokens @ $0.001)

solution_9.name: Git state validation
solution_9.approach: Force validation check before proceeding to next task

solution_10.name: Habit loop (behavioral)
solution_10.approach: Condition commit action with explicit reward framing

per_solution: Create distinct test files to trigger forgetting scenario
Run 20 sequential changes without active signaling
test_methodology.success_metric: Percentage of changes that result in committed code
